headline: Target books strongest sales growth in 4 years with customers buying into refreshed line up
summary: Target, which embarked on a turnaround plan under its new CEO three months ago, reported its largest jump in comparable sales in four years Wednesday.  More customers buying in all six of Target’s main merchandising categories helped deliver the better-than-expected sales, the company said.  Comparable sales — those coming from stores and digital channels operating for at least 12 months, rose 5.6% in the three-month period ended May 2.

content.title: Target books strongest sales growth in 4 years with customers buying into refreshed line up
content.summary: Target, which embarked on a turnaround plan under its new CEO three months ago, reported its largest jump in comparable sales in four years Wednesday.  More customers buying in all six of Target’s main merchandising categories helped deliver the better-than-expected sales, the company said.  Comparable sales — those coming from stores and digital channels operating for at least 12 months, rose 5.6% in the three-month period ended May 2.

headline: Target (NYSE:TGT) Surprises With Strong Q1 CY2026
summary: General merchandise retailer Target (NYSE:TGT) reported revenue ahead of Wall Street’s expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 6.7% year on year to $25.44 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $1.71 per share was 17.3% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Target books strongest sales growth in 4 years with customers buying into refreshed lineup
summary: Target reported the largest jump in comparable sales in four years Wednesday, but a cautious outlook overshadowed convincing evidence that changes under  the company's new CEO are resonating with customers.  Customers spent money across all of Target’s main merchandising categories and helped deliver better-than-expected sales.  Comparable sales — those coming from stores and digital channels operating for at least 12 months, rose 5.6% in the three-month period ended May 2.

Comparable sales — those coming from stores and digital channels operating for at least 12 months, rose 5.6% in the three-month period ended May 2.

headline: What's driving toy purchases when everything else is so expensive?
summary: Hasbro (HAS) CEO Chris Cocks sits down with Yahoo Finance Executive Editor Brian Sozzi for a conversation on what's perpetuating consumer spending on toys and collectibles while gas prices (RB=F), grocery bills, and the costs of eating out remain so elevated.  The toy company is best known for brands like Transformers, My Little Pony, Magic: The Gathering, and Dungeons & Dragons.

content.title: What's driving toy purchases when everything else is so expensive?
content.description:
Hasbro (HAS) CEO Chris Cocks sits down with Yahoo Finance Executive Editor Brian Sozzi for a conversation on what's perpetuating consumer spending on toys and collectibles while gas prices (RB=F), grocery bills, and the costs of eating out remain so elevated.

The toy company is best known for brands like Transformers, My Little Pony, Magic: The Gathering, and Dungeons & Dragons.
content.summary: Hasbro (HAS) CEO Chris Cocks sits down with Yahoo Finance Executive Editor Brian Sozzi for a conversation on what's perpetuating consumer spending on toys and collectibles while gas prices (RB=F), grocery bills, and the costs of eating out remain so elevated.  The toy company is best known for brands like Transformers, My Little Pony, Magic: The Gathering, and Dungeons & Dragons.

headline: Target leadership under fire over ‘strategic missteps’ on DEI, ICE
summary: Investors Trillium Asset Management, SOC Investment Group and Mercy Investment Services asked shareholders to oppose the reelection of Brian Cornell and Christine Leahy at Target’s annual shareholder meeting.

content.title: Target leadership under fire over ‘strategic missteps’ on DEI, ICE
content.summary: Investors Trillium Asset Management, SOC Investment Group and Mercy Investment Services asked shareholders to oppose the reelection of Brian Cornell and Christine Leahy at Target’s annual shareholder meeting.

headline: Target’s CX investments lead to 3-year highs in store metrics
summary: The retailer’s first quarter results beat expectations, but there is more to be done before Target has “the most delightful shopping experience in retail,” CEO Michael Fiddelke said.

content.title: Target’s CX investments lead to 3-year highs in store metrics
content.summary: The retailer’s first quarter results beat expectations, but there is more to be done before Target has “the most delightful shopping experience in retail,” CEO Michael Fiddelke said.

thumbnail.caption: People shop at a Target store on April 2, 2025, in New York City, N.Y. Target stores have seen a decline in sales since the company announced it would end its diversity, equity, and inclusion program in late January.
